recommendations.description: Fix encryption rule to check actual encryption enabled status, not just KMS key existence
recommendations.impact: HIGH - Ensures rule validates correct compliance requirement

critical_issues.description: Encryption rule checks if KMS key exists, not if encryption is actually enabled
critical_issues.problem: Encryption rule checks if KMS key exists, not if encryption is actually enabled
critical_issues.impact: HIGH - Rule may not be checking correct field or configuration
critical_issues.recommendation: Verify correct field/operator usage to check actual encryption enabled status, not just KMS key existence

cross_service_analysis.note: Note: All methods used are fsx service methods
cross_service_analysis.explanation: All python_methods (describe_file_systems, describe_snapshots, describe_backups) belong to fsx service. No cross-service issues found.
cross_service_analysis.recommendation: No action needed - rules correctly use fsx API methods

yaml_structure.field_paths: All field paths match emit structure

encryption_at_rest_enabled.issue: Rule checks if KmsKeyId exists, not if encryption is actually enabled
encryption_at_rest_enabled.recommended_check: KmsKeyId existence is actually a reasonable proxy - when encryption is enabled, KmsKeyId is populated. When not enabled, it may be null.
encryption_at_rest_enabled.impact: LOW - KmsKeyId existence is a valid proxy for encryption enabled status in FSX
encryption_at_rest_enabled.note: In AWS FSX, when encryption at rest is enabled, the KmsKeyId field is populated with the KMS key ID. When encryption is not enabled, KmsKeyId is typically null or not present. Therefore, checking for KmsKeyId existence is a reasonable way to verify encryption is enabled. However, this is an indirect check rather than a direct encryption status field.

windows_configuration_rules.issue: Rules check WindowsConfiguration fields, which only exist for Windows filesystems
windows_configuration_rules.recommended_check: Should filter for Windows filesystems only (FileSystemType equals WINDOWS) or handle non-Windows filesystems gracefully
windows_configuration_rules.impact: MEDIUM - Rules may fail or behave unexpectedly for non-Windows filesystems
windows_configuration_rules.note: These rules check WindowsConfiguration fields which only exist for Windows filesystems. For Lustre, ONTAP, or OpenZFS filesystems, WindowsConfiguration will be null. The rules should either filter for Windows filesystems only or handle null WindowsConfiguration gracefully.

notes.validation_approach: Validated YAML structure, field paths, discovery chains, and operators. All structural elements are correct.
notes.functional_validation: Tested against 5 AWS accounts - 0 execution errors. YAML structure is correct.
notes.encryption_check: KmsKeyId existence check is a reasonable proxy for encryption enabled status in FSX, though it's an indirect check.
notes.windows_configuration: Rules checking WindowsConfiguration fields should ideally filter for Windows filesystems or handle null values gracefully.
notes.overall_assessment: YAML implementation is structurally correct and executes without errors. Field paths match emit structure. Discovery chains are properly configured. Some rules have limitations that are documented for future enhancement.
